pages.full_text:
Half-term at Arrog Camp near Harbach, Mar
Simon 39, Eve 35, Thomas 9, William 7,
Peter 6 (guest) John 3 [illegible], Charles 6 weeks
lovely campsite in the hills.
Rubber tyre for the boys to swing on.
East wind tent.
We forgot the can opener.
The boys played ball with [illegible] family,
including man in a wheelchair.
Eve bodged into a wall.
Simon carried John in his push chair
all the way up to the castle at Harbach [?strom]
the carpet. The van worked beautifully. Hot
showers, comfortable bed.
We visited the river for swimming,
Harbach castle, sandy beach (including solids
icecream) and had a picnic at the Hodden
Steps.
On walks, Simon carried John on his
pages.notes: Page number 2 visible at top left. 'Half-term' suggests Easter or autumn break but no specific year confirmed. 'Harbach' spelling uncertain - could be variant of a German location name. '[?strom]' after Harbach uncertain reading. One or two words illegible in family name reference.

pages.full_text:
carrot, Thomas carried the food
beef sandwiches, apple, choc bar, water
and William sometimes carried the
spare clothes.
Almost Continuous work, glorious sunshine
a fabulous holiday; - even time to
read & write (letters)

Simon was very nervous about Eve's
driving, and only stopped concentrating for
a moment Peter into a "blood" sweat ca
the back seat ...

Haltern old boys' day. A very useful base
here. Joined Robert [?], Mt. [?]ll at
[illegible]
pages.notes: Continuation of left page narrative. 'Almost Continuous' appears to be intentional (not a typo for 'Almost Continuous'). Several words/phrases illegible: one name after 'Robert', unclear abbreviation 'Mt.', and partial text 'sweat ca' and final line. Date 'June 10th' visible but no page number visible at top. Faint pencil notations visible in margins.
